tests.name: init_sh_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh hung at Docker install prompt (Skip is index 3 but heredoc rejected with repeated 'Invalid choice. Enter a number between 1 and 4.'). Heredoc driver fragile per runbook expectation. Fell back to manual skeleton creation mimicking init.sh outputs (templates copied, .claude state files created, framework cloned, scripts copied, fake remote added).

tests.name: phase_state_json_phase_zero
tests.details: .current_phase=0 initially (later advanced to 2 for upgrade scenario)

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh has no scriptable mode for UAT — heredoc driver fragile
framework_bugs.reproduction: Pipe canned answers via heredoc to bash $FRAMEWORK/init.sh. The Docker install prompt offers options 1-3 (Colima/Docker Desktop/Skip) on macOS but rejects '3' with 'Invalid choice. Enter a number between 1 and 4.' indefinitely until the script is killed. The Docker prompt's allowed-range error message contradicts the actual offered range — message says 1-4 while the prompt only lists 3 options.
framework_bugs.expected: Either a non-interactive flag (e.g., --non-interactive or --answers-file) or consistent prompt-range messaging so heredoc drivers can determine the correct input.
framework_bugs.actual: init.sh loops on Docker prompt with mismatched range message; no scriptable bypass exists. Forces UAT agents to fall back to manual skeleton creation.

documentation_gaps.gap: init.sh lacks a scriptable / non-interactive mode (--non-interactive, --config FILE, etc.). Documented as expected gap in runbook line 116.
Docker install prompt error message says 'Enter a number between 1 and 4' but the prompt only lists 3 options (Colima, Docker Desktop, Skip).

tests.name: build_loop_baseline_built
tests.details: Built personal/standard baseline at phase=2 with one recorded feature 'uat-feat-baseline' (all 6 build_loop steps complete, recorded in build-progress.json).

tests.name: upgrade_command_exits_zero
tests.details: bash scripts/intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment organizational completed with exit 0 after tool-preferences.json + intake-progress.json were present (see bug below about missing fallback to phase-state.json).

tests.name: process_state_preserved
tests.details: .build_loop.feature=uat-feat-baseline preserved; steps_completed array unchanged; build-progress.json features_completed=[uat-feat-baseline] preserved

tests.name: new_required_artifacts_listed
tests.details: Post-upgrade validation listed missing PRODUCT_MANIFESTO.md, PROJECT_BIBLE.md, CHANGELOG.md as Phase 2 expectations and 3 hook-registration manual actions

tests.name: pending_approval_sentinel_respected
tests.details: Upgrade re-run with .claude/pending-approval.json present — script proceeded to validation phase but reported 'No changes needed — project is already at standard/organizational' (idempotent), so sentinel respect was not exercised by changes. Did not regress / blast through.

tests.name: upgrade_changelog_present_in_script
tests.details: scripts/upgrade-project.sh header lines 12-19 mention BL-006 (2026-04-24) pre-commit gate and BL-015 (2026-04-25) pending-approval sentinel

framework_bugs.reproduction: Run init.sh to create a project (does NOT write .claude/intake-progress.json — only intake-wizard.sh does). Then run scripts/intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment organizational. Output: '[FAIL] Invalid target track:  __TRACK__'.
framework_bugs.expected: upgrade-project.sh should fall back to .claude/phase-state.json (which DOES carry .track and .deployment, populated by init.sh) when neither tool-preferences.json nor intake-progress.json supplies the track.
framework_bugs.actual: Script reads CURRENT_TRACK from PROJECT_INTAKE.md placeholders ('__TRACK__') when both JSON files are missing/blank, then fails validation with the placeholder string. phase-state.json is never consulted for track despite being the canonical post-init state file.

framework_bugs.summary: Upgrade does not synchronize .claude/phase-state.json deployment field
framework_bugs.reproduction: On a personal/standard project at phase=2, run scripts/intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment organizational. After success, inspect .claude/phase-state.json — .deployment is still 'personal' even though .claude/intake-progress.json was correctly updated to 'organizational' and .last_upgrade was added.
framework_bugs.expected: phase-state.json.deployment should be updated alongside intake-progress.json so downstream consumers (validate.sh, check-phase-gate.sh, etc.) see consistent state.
framework_bugs.actual: phase-state.json retains deployment=personal post-upgrade; only last_upgrade timestamp is added. State drifts between phase-state.json (canonical phase tracker) and intake-progress.json (intake answers).

documentation_gaps.gap: No scriptable --upgrade-deployment path produces private_poc directly. Personal -> private_poc is not a documented transition: --upgrade-deployment organizational lifts to organizational/production (no POC mode), and there's no --to-private-poc flag analogous to --to-sponsored-poc. Runbook scenario 'private_poc (target)' for upgrade agents does not map cleanly onto an upgrade-project.sh command.

init.sh does not write .claude/intake-progress.json, but upgrade-project.sh treats it as a primary source of truth for track/deployment/poc_mode. Either init.sh should seed intake-progress.json with the answers it just collected, or upgrade-project.sh should read from phase-state.json.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: git ls-files | xargs grep for password/api_key/secret/token returned only documentation strings, GitHub Actions ${{ secrets.* }} references in release.yml, gitleaks descriptions, and platform-module example placeholders — no real credentials

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: N/A — .claude/process-audit.log absent because no FORCE/reset events occurred during run

summary: Upgrade flow personal -> organizational succeeds and preserves phase/process state, but two High-severity bugs surfaced: init.sh has no scriptable mode (fragile heredoc rejected at Docker prompt with mismatched range message) and upgrade-project.sh fails when intake-progress.json absent because it never falls back to phase-state.json which is the file init.sh actually writes; plus a Medium bug that phase-state.json.deployment is not synchronized post-upgrade.
